title: boolean OR collapse
summary: A complex OR expression can incorrectly evaluate to match even when both sides should be false.

title: 3VL logical ops with NULL
summary: Logical expressions involving NULL boolean fields violate expected three-valued logic semantics.

title: JSON NULL treated as TRUE in !=
summary: A JSON not-equal comparison can incorrectly treat NULL or missing values as true instead of unknown.

title: division by zero crash
summary: An arithmetic expression involving division by zero can crash or destabilize Milvus.

title: implicit downcast precision leakage
summary: Float expressions can be implicitly downcast to float32, changing comparison truth values near precision boundaries.

title: unvalidated index params poison collection
summary: Large or malicious index parameters can be accepted and then leave the collection in a bad state.

title: int64 overflow evaluation
summary: Arithmetic on large int64 values can overflow during expression evaluation and produce wrong filter results.

title: 3VL complex logical ops
summary: A complex NOT/AND/OR expression with nullable fields can lose rows that should remain under three-valued logic.

title: int64 to float precision loss in JSON
summary: Large int64 values inside JSON can lose precision and alias during comparison or membership checks.

title: parser rejects valid JSON expr
summary: The parser can reject a valid JSON expression when it is combined with other boolean structure.

title: signed zero with inverted index
summary: Indexed float filtering can mishandle +0.0 and -0.0, violating IEEE 754 expectations.

title: int64 closed-range aliasing
summary: Closed-range filtering on very large integers can alias neighboring values due to precision loss.

title: geo bounding box boundary
summary: A point on the exact geo bounding box boundary can be handled inconsistently.

title: float32 minimum boundary filtering
summary: Filtering at the float32 minimum finite value can be wrong for ==, <, <=, >, and >=, with REST/gRPC inconsistencies.

title: OBJECT is_none false negative
summary: Filtering an OBJECT field with is_none(False) can produce false negatives when explicit null is involved.

title: negative PQ segments validation bypass
summary: Schema creation can accept invalid negative PQ segment parameters instead of rejecting them.

title: signed zero approximate zero filtering
summary: Number filters can mishandle +0.0 and -0.0 around approximate zero comparisons.
